hypotheses.label: naive's blocking-side corpus saves seeds that carry a well-formed XML document skeleton (the <?xml ...?> + <!DOCTYPE declaration + a real '&...;' reference token) that the parser can advance through to reach xmlParseReference->xmlAddChild, whereas naive_ngram4's saved seeds are malformed surface fragments (URL/attribute garbage, repeated-quote runs) that lack the structural skeleton tokens the deep reference path requires

params.note: STRUCTURAL well-formedness tokens of an XML document head + reference, NOT byte-offset literals. winner_token_count = median over naive resolving seeds of how many distinct skeleton tokens each carries; loser_token_count = same over naive_ngram4 blocking seeds. token_lift = winner_token_count / max(loser_token_count, eps). Per-seed median, so unequal arm sizes do not confound (the confound that sank corpus_size_ratio). libxml2 owning server = sB (sB corpora = lcms,libxml2,libpng,bloaty); seeds live in db/blockers.sqlite resolving_seeds/blocking_seeds for this rep.

hypotheses.prediction: G1: the analysis directly observes the seed-composition asymmetry (winner seed offsets 0x1c-0x3f = 'ion="1.0"?>.<!DO' = a clean <?xml...?>+<!DOCTYPE head; loser seed offsets 0x00-0x1f = 'ef="http://fdkeu' = attribute/URL garbage with NO document head). If well-formedness is the mechanism, naive's resolving seeds carry the XML skeleton tokens (<?xml/?>/<!DOCTYPE/&/;) at a far higher per-seed rate than naive_ngram4's blocking seeds => token_lift >= 2.0 AND winner_token_richer == true. The discriminated ALTERNATIVE this rules out: if the divergence were purely a runtime energy/reach effect with NO seed-composition difference (both arms' saved seeds equally carry the document skeleton, token_lift ~ 1), then the gap would be the already-tried depth_reach story (which did NOT hold for this branch) or pure stochastic noise -- NOT a well-formedness mechanism. This is a GENUINELY DIFFERENT measurement from the refuted depth_reach (runtime per-seed gate-line reach) and the superseded corpus_size_ratio (whole-campaign corpus SCALE): token_count is a per-seed STRUCTURAL-COMPOSITION property of each arm's saved corpus, attributable to this branch's two seed sets, and it can come out either way. G2-independent: it inspects what bytes/tokens each arm's seeds CONTAIN, never the resolve pattern that defined the shape.

hypotheses.label: naive's blocking-side corpus actually drives the deep xmlParseReference->xmlAddChild call chain to the null-parent gate true-side per seed, while naive_ngram4's corpus stays mired in shallow tokenizer states and never reaches it

params.note: winner_fz/loser_fz map to resolving_seeds(naive)/blocking_seeds(naive_ngram4) in db; libxml2 cov image = docker/targets/Dockerfile.libxml2.cov (owning server = sB)

hypotheses.prediction: G1: when each arm's saved seeds are replayed through the libxml2 cov binary, naive's resolving seeds take the BLOCKED true-side of tree.c:3421 (the analysis observed L3421 T=18) at a far higher per-seed rate than naive_ngram4's blocking seeds (analysis: ngram4 L3421 true=0, false=194) => side_lift >> 1 AND winner_deeper == true. The discriminated ALTERNATIVE: if the divergence were mere stochastic energy noise rather than a structural deep-path-reach gap, both arms' corpora would reach the gate true-side comparably (side_lift ~ 1, winner_deeper == false). The measurement can come out either way, so it is falsifiable AND it separates a genuine path-reach mechanism from a non-path artifact. G2-independent: depth_reach measures what each arm's CORPUS reaches at the gate line, NOT the resolve pattern that defined the shape.

hypotheses.label: ngram-4 feedback floods the corpus with near-duplicate shallow-tokenizer seeds, starving energy from the rare deep xmlParseReference->xmlAddChild path that naive reaches (PRIOR; corpus-SCALE energy story)
hypotheses.prediction: G1: naive_ngram4 corpus is inflated with shallow-gram entries (corpus_size_ratio >> 1) while naive reaches deeper. A non-saturation mechanism predicts comparable corpus sizes.
hypotheses.non_decidable_reason: REFUTED-as-non-discriminable in round 0 and NOT re-tested: corpus_size_ratio measures a whole-campaign corpus-SCALE / energy-allocation property that cannot be attributed to this single branch (it cannot separate ngram saturation-starvation from plain non-mutation of the '&...;' bytes for ONE branch, and n=1 gives no cross-branch threshold support). The per-branch deep-path-reach question this prior was really asking is captured instead by ngram_deep_path_reach_gap (depth_reach) and the new ngram_wellformed_skeleton_token_gap (token_count). Kept here only as the documented refuted/superseded record.

data_realities_respected: Cross-server (rule #4): libxml2 is owned by server B (sB corpora = lcms,libxml2,libpng,bloaty); NOT marked decidable:false merely because libxml2 is absent from the s4 on-disk set (curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3). Both the new token_count test and the kept depth_reach test are designed to run on the OWNING server, which has libxml2 corpora + the cov image (docker/targets/Dockerfile.libxml2.cov).

new token_count descriptor (seed_set:token_count:tokens:per_branch) needs resolving_seeds(naive) + blocking_seeds(naive_ngram4) in db/blockers.sqlite for branch 6939; the rep set carries these. It counts per-seed STRUCTURAL XML skeleton tokens (<?xml/<!DOCTYPE/&/;), then takes the per-seed median per arm, so unequal arm sizes do not confound (this is the per-seed fix for the corpus_size_ratio scale confound).

token_count tokens are STRUCTURAL well-formedness markers, NOT fixed-offset value literals -- the gate is presence/non-null structural (parent==NULL), so byte_freq_ratio (fixed-offset value gate) stays inapplicable and is NOT proposed.

no seed_lineage 'ngram event' proposed -- ngram-4 is a coverage FEEDBACK, untagged in mutation_op (analogous to the value_profile feedback invisibility); its effect is measured only via what each arm's corpus CONTAINS / REACHES, never via a lineage event.

joint_necessity (seed_set:struct_size_and_token_count:tokens:per_branch) NOT reused as-is: it is scoped to the joint vpc-needs-both ASSEMBLY-DEPTH subtype with size_lift/vp_tags/cmp_tags/vpc_tags metrics over a vpc/cmp/vp seed triad -- wrong shape (this is a naive-vs-naive_ngram4 anti pair, no vpc/vp arm). The token-presence idea is borrowed but re-expressed as a fresh seed_set:token_count descriptor over the naive/naive_ngram4 arms.
corpus_size_ratio (the prior) remains the genuinely non-attributable whole-campaign scale property; it stays decidable:false and is superseded, not relaxed.
notes: target=libxml2 (owning server = sB). Round-2 PIVOT. Round 1 tried ngram_deep_path_reach_gap (depth_reach, runtime per-seed gate-line reach) and the rule did NOT hold for libxml2/6939 (rule_not_met); it is KEPT verbatim per the superset/monotonic invariant (and re-validates any future confirmed branch), but it left this branch UNLABELED. NEW mechanism = ngram_wellformed_skeleton_token_gap, tried FIRST: a genuinely DIFFERENT measurement from the refuted depth_reach. depth_reach asked 'does the corpus REACH the gate line deeper per seed at RUNTIME?'; token_count asks 'do the winner's saved seeds CONTAIN the XML document-skeleton tokens (<?xml/<!DOCTYPE/&/;) that the deep reference path requires, which the malformed loser seeds lack?' -- a per-seed STRUCTURAL-COMPOSITION property of each arm's corpus, attributable to this branch's two seed sets. This is exactly the seed-composition asymmetry the analysis evidence_trail records directly (winner seed 'ion="1.0"?>.<!DO' clean head vs loser seed 'ef="http://fdkeu' garbage; what_input_feature: 'requires a well-formed-enough document skeleton'). G1: rules out the alternative that the gap is pure runtime energy/reach with no seed-content difference (which depth_reach already failed to confirm). G3 honesty: ngram is a non-local feedback family, often legitimately non-discriminable; token_count is a real, runnable, falsifiable per-seed measurement, so it is decidable:true, but if the owning server finds the two arms' seeds carry the skeleton tokens comparably (token_lift ~ 1) OR the naive_ngram4 blocking seeds are missing from the DB, the branch honestly routes to fallback inconclusive -- I do NOT force a label. The corpus_size_ratio prior stays decidable:false (whole-campaign scale, non-attributable to one branch), superseded by the two per-branch tests, not relaxed.
